H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78) is strong (duplicate entry for consistency). See H‑OS‑L1 above.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: One of the most used and well‑liked protocols; helps people unwind after overload, especially during commutes.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73) is strong. Scores cluster near 7–8. Qualitative responses (21) are chiefly self‑listeners (11); music is liked by 10 and neutral by 6, rhythm split evenly. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, essentially matching the observed mean. Distribution is unimodal.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Steady and calming, helping busy minds settle at their own pace.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68) is meaningful. Scores concentrate around 7–8 with low variance. Qualitative data (23) show self‑listeners (15) and high music liking (11); rhythm is equally balanced and very settling. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, slightly lower than observed, suggesting modest over‑performance. No clear bimodality.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Great for winding down before sleep, though a few find the rhythm distracting at first.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: The Meadow protocol (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93) carries a strong confidence tier. Its score distribution shows a modest right‑skew with modes around 8–9. Qualitative responses (23) are dominated by self‑listeners (13) and clinicians (6); 16 report liking or loving the music and 17 note a balanced or very settling rhythm, with settle times clustered at 5–10 minutes. YouTube reach is limited (7 views) and retention data are unavailable. AutoGluon predicts 7.84 for the anxiety zone, about 0.6 points higher than observed, hinting at untapped potential. No clear bimodality is evident.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners feel calmer after a short session, especially when dealing with anxiety.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62) reaches meaningful confidence. Scores cluster tightly around 7–8 with low variance. Among 20 one‑minute respondents, self‑listeners predominate (12) and music opinions are split between liked (9) and neutral (5); rhythm is generally balanced or very settling. Views are very low (6) and no retention metrics exist. AutoGluon’s predicted anxiety score (7.84) exceeds the observed mean by ~0.9, suggesting room for improvement. Distribution shows a small gap at low scores, hinting at a possible responder subgroup.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: It helps ease stress, though some hear it as subtle background during therapy or commuting.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89) is classified as meaningful. The distribution is spread with a modest peak near 8 and a long tail to higher scores. Qualitative data (20) highlight a mix of self‑listeners (11) and caregivers (6); 13 report noticing improvement and rhythm is described as very settling by most. YouTube views are minimal (4). AutoGluon forecasts 7.84, roughly 0.8 points above the mean, indicating latent efficacy. No pronounced bimodality is detected.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Provides a gentle anxiety lift; quiet settings make the effect clearer.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70) carries meaningful confidence. Scores lean toward the lower end with a noticeable left tail. Qualitative feedback (24) shows strong improvement ratings (significant for 8 respondents) despite low scores; music is largely liked (11) and rhythm balanced/very settling (17). YouTube reach is low (6 views). AutoGluon predicts a crisis zone score of 7.96, markedly higher (+1.6) than the observed mean, flagging a potential delivery or entrainment mismatch. The distribution is relatively smooth without clear bimodality.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: During intense moments many report quick relief, but overall scores are modest.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70) is meaningful. Scores cluster around 6–7 with a modest spread. Qualitative responses (16) are mixed; 6 report moderate improvement and 5 notice improvement, while music opinions are split between neutral (6) and loved (5). Rhythm is described as very settling by 8 respondents. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon’s crisis prediction (7.96) exceeds observed mean by ~1.4 points, suggesting the protocol could benefit from timing or hold‑phase tweaks. No obvious bimodality.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Calms crisis feelings, though experiences vary and some find the rhythm less engaging.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91) is meaningful. Distribution hints at a bimodal pattern with clusters near mid‑range and higher scores. Qualitative data (19) report quick settle times (7 within the first minutes) and mixed music opinions (7 liked, 4 not liked). Rhythm is mainly balanced. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.96 for crisis, again above observed mean, reinforcing the possibility of a sub‑group effect. The subtle bimodality warrants subgroup analysis.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Works fast for some, but the response is split, hinting at different listener types.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86) is strong. Scores spread with modes at 7–8 and 9–10. Qualitative feedback (15) is mostly self‑listeners (13); music liked/loved by 13, rhythm balanced or very settling. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for sleep, close to observed. Notable drift (sim = 0.530) flags possible analytic shift.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Helps people relax quickly, often used in therapy or study sessions.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55) is meaningful and has the lowest std in HEALING. Scores cluster tightly 6–9. Qualitative input (15) is clinician‑heavy (6); music receives mixed opinions (5 liked, 6 neutral) and rhythm is noted as “Too busy” by 3, a unique flag. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, aligning closely. The “Did NOT settle” signal in qualitative data suggests possible entrainment difficulty.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed reactions – many like it, but some think the rhythm is too active for sleep.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68) is meaningful. Scores are centered near 7 with a modest tail. Qualitative responses (22) are primarily self‑listeners (12); music liked by 8, rhythm balanced by 9 but also “Slightly busy” by 7. YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, matching. No major anomalies beyond the occasional “Did NOT settle” remark.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Gently soothing, best when paired with a regular bedtime routine.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82) is meaningful. Scores are lower than other focus protocols; many report slight improvement. Qualitative data (23) show self‑listeners (12) and mixed rhythm comfort (9 slightly busy). YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, a sizable (+1.3) gap suggesting the protocol may benefit from redesign or stronger routing.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Aids concentration but can feel a bit busy for some listeners.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60) is meaningful. Scores improve to the series’ higher range. Qualitative responses (17) note moderate to significant improvement and music loved by 5. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, aligning closely.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Improves clarity, especially in supportive care settings, though underused.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97) is meaningful and the top focus protocol. Scores are high with 71% positive; qualitative feedback (29) emphasizes significant improvement. YouTube views are 15, still modest despite strong outcomes. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, essentially matching. The low view count suggests under‑exposure.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: The most effective focus tool; many report sharp, clear thinking afterward.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73) is meaningful. Distribution leans left with many low scores; slight improvement is most common. Qualitative feedback (21) shows self‑listeners (13) and quick settle times (6 within first minutes). Music is liked by 9, rhythm balanced. YouTube views are 2. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for focus, a 1‑point boost, indicating possible under‑realization.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Offers a mild focus boost, though many notice only slight changes.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41) is meaningful. Scores tighten around 7; improvement ratings include significant for 5 respondents. Qualitative data (15) highlight self‑listeners (10) with music loved by 6. YouTube views are 2. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, modestly higher (+0.9).
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Smoothly supports creative flow, especially when listened to for longer periods.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32) is developing. Scores are highest in the creative‑flow set. Qualitative feedback (25) is diverse with self‑listeners (10) and notable significant improvements (6). YouTube reach is 7. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, close to observed. Drift is high (sim = 0.602), marking this as a semantic outlier.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Stronger creative lift, but still early data; clearer guidance may help.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34) is meaningful. Scores cluster near 7; qualitative input (23) shows mixed settle times, many at 20 minutes. YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for maintenance, about +0.7, indicating potential benefit from earlier entrainment.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Supports daily balance, though it takes time to feel its effect.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03) is meaningful with higher variance. Scores range widely; qualitative data (16) are split between caregivers and self‑listeners. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, a notable (+1.0) gap, suggesting redesign of rhythmic profile.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Feedback is mixed; rhythm works for some and feels off for others.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73) is developing. Scores modestly above 6; qualitative feedback (19) includes 4 “Did NOT settle” reports, indicating entrainment issues. YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, again higher, pointing to a possible need for stronger hold phases.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Small sample, but some benefit; a few users didn’t settle.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94) is meaningful. Scores hover around 7; qualitative feedback (22) is strongly positive (10 loved, 9 liked) and rhythm balanced. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts a top‑zone low‑affect score of 8.23, substantially above observed, marking this as a high‑potential cross‑bucket protocol.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Boosts mood and energy over time, especially in low‑energy states.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71) is meaningful. Scores similar to L1; qualitative data (15) note significant improvement for 7 respondents and music loved by 6. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, a notable uplift.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Loved by many for mood lift, especially before sleep or therapy.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74) is developing with the lowest mean among mood & energy. Qualitative feedback (25) is mixed; rhythm is balanced but some report no effect. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, indicating potential if the protocol is refined.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Less effective than earlier levels; some feel nothing while others get mild energy.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01) is meaningful. Scores are widely spread; qualitative responses (17) note quick settle times (5 min) and mixed improvement. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon routes this protocol to low‑affect zones with a predicted score of 8.23, well above observed, suggesting latent potential if ascent pacing is adjusted.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Gives a gentle motivation lift and settles fast, but could help more with earlier exposure.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63) is developing. Scores cluster low; qualitative feedback (21) shows moderate improvement but longer settle times (20 min). YouTube views are 7. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, indicating a mismatch that may be addressed by slowing the ascent curve.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Provides a slow energy boost; best for gradual rise rather than quick fixes.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38) is developing with a high mean but large variance. Qualitative input (17) reports moderate improvement; drift is extreme (sim = 0.507). YouTube views are 7. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, suggesting the protocol could be valuable if variance is reduced.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Surprisingly strong motivation despite low data; may be under‑recognized.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62) is meaningful. Scores centre around 7; qualitative feedback (25) is positive (9 loved, 9 liked) and rhythm balanced. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for mindclarity, about +0.8, indicating room for optimization.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Helps clarify thoughts; most listeners enjoy the music and rhythm.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64) is developing. Distribution is tighter; qualitative data (20) report significant improvement for 7 respondents and very settling rhythm. YouTube views are 12. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a modest boost.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Well‑received for focus and calm; users settle quickly.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45) is developing and shows the lowest mean across the dataset. The distribution is highly dispersed with peaks at both extremes, indicating a possible bimodal subgroup. Qualitative feedback (17) is mixed, with 9 noticing improvement but 5 neutral; rhythm is sometimes described as too busy. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, substantially higher (+1.8), flagging this protocol for redesign or alternative routing.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Harder to connect with – some like it, others find it too busy or quiet.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across the three series the data show generally consistent therapeutic benefit, with HEALING delivering the highest average scores and tightest spread, THRIVING achieving strong peaks in focus protocols, and TRANSFORMING exhibiting the widest variance as expected for low‑affect designs.
• HEALING’s mean (6.98) exceeds the other series and its variance (1.77) is the lowest.
• THRIVING shows the highest individual protocol means, especially T‑FC‑L3 (7.63).
• TRANSFORMING’s lower mean (6.59) and higher variance (1.87) reflect its ascending‑arc intent.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
HEALING protocols, predominantly DESCENT, display stable outcomes with modest variance, supporting nervous‑system regulation aims.
THRIVING’s HOLD designs produce robust focus and clarity scores, with a noticeable dose‑response across L1‑L3 levels.
TRANSFORMING’s ASCENT arcs generate the most spread, highlighting the challenge of engaging low‑affect states.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols show mismatches between observed outcomes and qualitative or predictive signals.
• X‑MC‑L3 records a low mean (5.7) and high std (2.45), suggesting a bimodal or mismatched listener population.
• H‑CR‑L3 displays a subtle bimodal distribution despite typical crisis‑protocol expectations.
• H‑SL‑L2 uniquely reports “Did NOT settle” responses, indicating possible entrainment issues.
• T‑FC‑L3 achieves strong outcome scores yet has limited YouTube reach (15 views), hinting at under‑exposure.
These patterns suggest sub‑group effects or delivery refinements merit investigation.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted adjustments could improve efficacy and reach across the library.
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] • X‑MC‑L3 (n=10) – redesign or reroute to better match listener sub‑groups due to low mean and high variance.
• H‑CR‑L1 (n=43) – explore faster entry or enhanced hold phase to close the gap between observed mean (6.35) and AutoGluon crisis prediction (7.96).
• X‑MD‑L1/L2 (n=22,14) – consider slowing the ascent arc or adding interim hold phases to raise low positive‑response rates.
• T‑FC‑L3 (n=35) – increase visibility (e.g., featured routing, YouTube promotion) to capitalize on high efficacy.
• H‑OS‑L1 (n=95) – amplify distribution channels to leverage strong outcomes and modest view count (30).
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals indicate notable evolution in model framing alongside stable protocol clusters.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 29 protocols show similarity < 0.85, with 14 < 0.75, marking substantial drift over the past months.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: high similarity pairs include T‑FC‑L1↔T‑FC‑L2 (0.878), H‑SL‑L1↔H‑SL‑L2 (0.869), and T‑CF‑L1↔T‑CF‑L2 (0.841); the most distinct are H‑OS‑L3, T‑FC‑L3 and X‑MC‑L3.
• Confidence note: drift metrics are based on five runs; while informative, they should be interpreted with moderate caution.

by_protocol.X-ME-L2: Sunrise (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71) is meaningful. Scores similar to L1; qualitative data (15) note significant improvement for 7 respondents and music loved by 6. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, a notable uplift.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: Updraft (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74) is developing with the lowest mean among mood & energy. Qualitative feedback (25) is mixed; rhythm is balanced but some report no effect. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, indicating potential if the protocol is refined.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most listeners feel calmer after a short session, especially when dealing with anxiety.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: It helps ease stress, though some hear it as subtle background during therapy or commuting.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Provides a gentle anxiety lift; quiet settings make the effect clearer.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: During intense moments many report quick relief, but overall scores are modest.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Calms crisis feelings, though experiences vary and some find the rhythm less engaging.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Works fast for some, but the response is split, hinting at different listener types.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: One of the most used and well‑liked protocols; helps people unwind after overload, especially during commutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Steady and calming, helping busy minds settle at their own pace.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Great for winding down before sleep, though a few find the rhythm distracting at first.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Helps people relax quickly, often used in therapy or study sessions.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Mixed reactions – many like it, but some think the rhythm is too active for sleep.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Gently soothing, best when paired with a regular bedtime routine.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Offers a mild focus boost, though many notice only slight changes.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Smoothly supports creative flow, especially when listened to for longer periods.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Stronger creative lift, but still early data; clearer guidance may help.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Aids concentration but can feel a bit busy for some listeners.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Improves clarity, especially in supportive care settings, though underused.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: The most effective focus tool; many report sharp, clear thinking afterward.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Supports daily balance, though it takes time to feel its effect.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Feedback is mixed; rhythm works for some and feels off for others.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Small sample, but some benefit; a few users didn’t settle.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Helps clarify thoughts; most listeners enjoy the music and rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Well‑received for focus and calm; users settle quickly.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Harder to connect with – some like it, others find it too busy or quiet.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Gives a gentle motivation lift and settles fast, but could help more with earlier exposure.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Provides a slow energy boost; best for gradual rise rather than quick fixes.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Surprisingly strong motivation despite low data; may be under‑recognized.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Boosts mood and energy over time, especially in low‑energy states.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Loved by many for mood lift, especially before sleep or therapy.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Less effective than earlier levels; some feel nothing while others get mild energy.
